name: vscode-ai
description: Visual Studio Code extension for Azure Machine Learning

colors.description: Generic color for tree node icons

azureML.schemasVersion.description: Version prefix of the schemas to show (i.e. 0.0.22-october-cand will show 0.0.22-october-cand/workspace.schema.json from the blob)

viewsWelcome.contents:
Thank you for using Azure Machine Learning extension in VS Code. To get the most out of the extension, please sign in using your Azure Account, or create a free Azure Account.
[Sign In to Azure...](command:azureml.internal.azureAccount.login)
[Create an Azure Account...](command:azureml.internal.azureAccount.create)
[Create an Azure for Students Account...](command:azureml.internal.azureAccount.createStudentsAccount)
Check out our quick start guide on how to use VS Code with Azure Machine Learning here: [Get Started with Azure ML in VS Code](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/how-to-launch-vs-code-remote?view=azureml-api-2&tabs=vscode-web)
Support for this extension is provided on our [GitHub Issue Tracker](https://github.com/Microsoft/vscode-tools-for-ai/issues). You can submit a bug report, a feature suggestion or participate in discussions.

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm install && npm run clean && npm run webpack && cd ./azureml-language-server && npm run vscode:prepublish
